content: Diagnosis by dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA) of lumbar spine and hip. WHO criteria based on T-score (comparison to young adult mean): normal ≥-1.0, osteopenia (low bone mass) -1.0 to -2.5, osteoporosis ≤-2.5. Z-score (age-matched) used in premenopausal women, men <50; Z-score ≤-2.0 suggests secondary cause. FRAX tool estimates 10-year fracture risk using clinical risk factors ± femoral neck BMD; treatment considered if 10-year hip fracture risk ≥3% or major osteoporotic fracture risk ≥20% (US thresholds). Indications for DEXA: women ≥65, men ≥70, younger postmenopausal women/adults 50-69 with risk factors, adults with fragility fracture, conditions/medications causing bone loss. Vertebral imaging (VFA or X-ray) if height loss, kyphosis, or suspected vertebral fracture. Laboratory evaluation to exclude secondary causes: CBC, CMP, calcium, phosphorus, 25-OH vitamin D, TSH, PTH, testosterone (men), celiac screen, SPEP/UPEP if indicated.
